# `.sacxpiorif` — Post-Op Instructions: ORIF

> [!tip] Usage
> Patient handout for post-ORIF care. Adjust diet and elastic instructions per case.

---

POST-OPERATIVE INSTRUCTIONS — JAW FRACTURE REPAIR (ORIF)

SWELLING: Significant swelling is expected, peaking at 48-72 hours. Ice packs 20 min on/off for the first 48 hours. After 48 hours, switch to warm moist compresses.

DIET: ___ (soft no-chew diet x 6 weeks / liquid/puree diet x 2 weeks then soft x 4 weeks / strict liquid diet while in MMF). Ensure adequate protein and calories (goal 2000+ kcal/day). Supplement with Ensure/Boost if needed.

ORAL HYGIENE: Peridex (chlorhexidine) rinse BID starting POD1. Gently brush accessible teeth with a soft, child-sized toothbrush. Use a curved-tip syringe with warm salt water to irrigate surgical sites.

MEDICATIONS: Take all medications as directed. Complete the full course of antibiotics.

ACTIVITY: No contact sports, heavy lifting (greater than 15 lbs), or strenuous exercise for 6 weeks. No forceful nose blowing (especially midface fractures). Sneeze with mouth open.

NUMBNESS: Facial numbness is common, particularly lower lip/chin. Usually improves over weeks to months; may be permanent in some cases.

HARDWARE: Titanium plates and screws are MRI-compatible. Typically left permanently unless symptomatic.

ELASTICS: ___ (wear guiding elastics at all times except eating and oral hygiene / N/A)

FOLLOW-UP: 1 week, then 2, 4, and 6 weeks post-op.

EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, uncontrolled bleeding, inability to open airway (if MMF), fever greater than 101.5°F, vision changes, rapidly expanding swelling — go to the ED.
